mcqs.question: What is the primary advantage of Randomized Select over sorting the entire array?

options.text: It finds the k-th element in O(n) average time vs O(n log n)
It always produces a sorted array
It can handle duplicate elements better

mcqs.explanation: Randomized Select achieves O(n) expected time complexity by only partitioning the necessary part of the array, whereas sorting requires O(n log n) time.

mcqs.question: Randomized Select is also known as:
mcqs.explanation: Randomized Select is popularly known as QuickSelect, named after its similarity to QuickSort, and also as Hoare's Selection Algorithm after its creator Charles Antony Richard Hoare.

mcqs.question: What is the expected time complexity of Randomized Select?
mcqs.explanation: Randomized Select has an expected time complexity of O(n) because on average, the algorithm eliminates half of the remaining elements in each partition step.

mcqs.question: What is the worst-case time complexity of Randomized Select?
mcqs.explanation: The worst-case O(n²) occurs when the random pivot selection consistently results in highly unbalanced partitions (e.g., always picking the smallest or largest element).

mcqs.question: Why does Randomized Select use a random pivot instead of always choosing the first element?

options.text: Random pivot is easier to implement
To avoid O(n²) worst-case and ensure expected O(n) performance
Random pivot gives better space complexity
It produces a more stable output

mcqs.explanation: Using a random pivot helps avoid worst-case scenarios that occur with fixed pivot strategies (like always picking the first element on a sorted array), ensuring expected O(n) performance.

mcqs.question: In Randomized Select, after partitioning, if the pivot index is greater than k-1, which partition should be searched?

options.text: Left partition with the same k

mcqs.explanation: If pivot index > k-1, the k-th element must be in the left partition (elements smaller than pivot), so we search there with the same value of k.

mcqs.question: How does the recursion in Randomized Select differ from QuickSort?

options.text: Randomized Select does not use recursion
Randomized Select recurses into both partitions
Randomized Select recurses into only one partition based on pivot position

mcqs.explanation: Unlike QuickSort which recursively processes both partitions, Randomized Select only recurses into one partition - the one containing the k-th element, saving approximately half the work.

mcqs.question: What is the space complexity of Randomized Select (considering the call stack)?
mcqs.explanation: The space complexity is O(log n) expected due to the average recursion depth, but O(n) in the worst case when the partitions are highly unbalanced.

mcqs.question: In 0-based indexing, what is the pivot index when we find the k-th smallest element in Randomized Select?
mcqs.explanation: In 0-based indexing, the k-th smallest element is at index k-1. So when the pivot lands at index k-1, we have found our element.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT a property of Randomized Select?
mcqs.explanation: Randomized Select is not a stable algorithm. The partitioning step can change the relative order of equal elements, unlike stable sorting algorithms.

mcqs.question: When finding the median using Randomized Select, what value of k should be used?
mcqs.explanation: For finding the median with 1-based indexing, use k = (n+1)/2 (lower median) or k = (n+2)/2 (upper median). With 0-based indexing, use (n-1)/2 or n/2.

mcqs.question: What is the main difference between deterministic selection (like Median of Medians) and Randomized Select?

options.text: Randomized Select has O(n²) worst-case while deterministic has O(n) worst-case
Deterministic selection has O(n²) worst-case while Randomized has O(n) expected

mcqs.explanation: Deterministic selection algorithms like Median of Medians guarantee O(n) worst-case time, while Randomized Select has O(n²) worst-case but O(n) expected time.

mcqs.question: In Randomized Select, if the pivot index is exactly k-1, what should be returned?
mcqs.explanation: When the pivot index equals k-1, the pivot element is exactly the k-th smallest element, so it is returned immediately without further recursion.

mcqs.question: Which algorithm is Randomized Select most similar to in terms of partitioning?
mcqs.explanation: Randomized Select uses the same partitioning procedure as QuickSort - dividing the array around a pivot element into elements less than and greater than the pivot.

mcqs.question: What happens when k equals 1 in Randomized Select?

options.text: It behaves like finding minimum in one pass

mcqs.explanation: When k=1 (finding the smallest element), Randomized Select performs a single partition and returns the minimum, similar to finding minimum in one linear scan.

mcqs.question: To find the k-th largest element instead of k-th smallest, what modification is needed in Randomized Select?

options.text: Use a different pivot selection strategy

mcqs.explanation: To find the k-th largest, use k' = n - k + 1 (converting to k-th smallest problem), or equivalently, search for the (n-k)th smallest element.

mcqs.question: What is the probability that Randomized Select achieves O(n log n) time?

options.text: Zero - it never takes that long
Very low - O(n log n) is not a typical runtime

mcqs.explanation: O(n log n) is not a typical runtime for Randomized Select. It either achieves O(n) expected or O(n²) worst-case. O(n log n) could occur in some intermediate scenarios but is unlikely.

mcqs.question: Which of these is a practical application of Randomized Select?

options.text: Finding order statistics like quartiles and percentiles

mcqs.explanation: Randomized Select is ideal for finding order statistics - k-th smallest/largest elements, medians, quartiles, percentiles, etc., where full sorting would be wasteful.

mcqs.question: In Randomized Select, after partitioning, if pivot index is less than k-1, how is the new k calculated for the right partition?
mcqs.explanation: When pivot index < k-1, the k-th element is in the right partition. The new k becomes k - pivot_index because we've effectively removed pivot_index elements from the left.

mcqs.question: Is Randomized Select suitable for finding the median of medians in an array?

options.text: Yes, it is the most efficient method
No, it cannot guarantee O(n) for this specific task
Only if the array is already sorted
It is the only method that can be used

mcqs.explanation: Randomized Select has O(n²) worst-case for finding the median, so deterministic algorithms like Median of Medians are preferred when guaranteed O(n) performance is required.
